### `ParamCliDef`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:43`*

```typescript
ParamCliDef
```

CLI-specific decoration for a parameter. All fields are optional — omit the entire `cli` key for params with no CLI surface.

#### `positional`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:49`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

When true, registers as `.argument('<name>')` (positional). When false or omitted, registers as `.option('--name <value>')`.  false

#### `short`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:55`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Short flag alias, e.g. `'-t'` for `--type`, `'-s'` for `--status`. Only meaningful when `positional` is false/omitted.

#### `flag`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:62`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Override the CLI flag name when it differs from the param's `name`. e.g. `name: 'includeArchive'` but `flag: 'include-archive'` Defaults to kebab-case of `name`.

#### `variadic`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:69`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

For array-type params on the CLI: when true the option can be repeated. When false/omitted, the CLI accepts a single comma-separated string.  false

#### `parse`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:74`*

```typescript
((value: string) => unknown) | undefined
```

Custom parse function applied by Commander (e.g. `parseInt`).

### `ParamDef`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:82`*

```typescript
ParamDef
```

A fully-described parameter definition.  One `ParamDef` entry drives Commander: `.argument()` (positional) or `.option()` (flag).

#### `name`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:84`*

```typescript
string
```

Canonical camelCase parameter name (matches the key in `params` dict).

#### `type`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:87`*

```typescript
ParamType
```

Runtime value type. Drives JSON Schema `type` and Commander parsing.

#### `required`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:92`*

```typescript
boolean
```

When true, Commander registers as a positional argument (`<name>` or `[name]`).

#### `description`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:95`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable description used in Commander help text.

#### `cli`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:98`*

```typescript
ParamCliDef | undefined
```

CLI-specific metadata. Omit entire key if this param has no CLI surface.

#### `enum`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:101`*

```typescript
readonly string[] | undefined
```

JSON Schema `enum` constraint for this parameter.

#### `hidden`

*Defined in `packages/cleo/src/dispatch/types.ts:108`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

When true, the parameter is excluded from schema generation. Use for CLI-only params (e.g. `--dry-run`, `--offset`).  false

### `ExternalTaskProvider`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task-sync.ts:197`*

```typescript
ExternalTaskProvider
```

Interface that provider adapters implement to expose their external task system to the reconciliation engine.  Provider-specific parsing lives in the adapter — core never sees native formats. Consumers implement this interface to integrate their issue tracker with CLEO.

**Examples**

```typescript
class LinearAdapter implements ExternalTaskProvider {
  async getExternalTasks(projectDir: string): Promise<ExternalTask[]> {
    const issues = await linearClient.issues({ projectId: '...' });
    return issues.map(issue => ({
      externalId: issue.id,
      title: issue.title,
      status: mapLinearStatus(issue.state),
      description: issue.description,
      priority: mapLinearPriority(issue.priority),
      labels: issue.labels.map(l => l.name),
      url: issue.url,
      providerMeta: { linearId: issue.identifier },
    }));
  }
}
```

#### `getExternalTasks()`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task-sync.ts:204`*

```typescript
(projectDir: string) => Promise<ExternalTask[]>
```

Read the provider's current task state and return normalized ExternalTasks.

**Parameters**

- `projectDir` — Project root directory.

**Returns**: Array of external tasks in normalized form.

#### `pushTaskState()`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task-sync.ts:213`*

```typescript
((tasks: ReadonlyArray<{ id: string; title: string; status: string; }>, projectDir: string) => Promise<void>) | undefined
```

Optionally push CLEO task state back to the provider (outbound sync). Not all providers support bidirectional sync.

**Parameters**

- `tasks` — Current CLEO tasks to push.
- `projectDir` — Project root directory.

### `Conduit`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/conduit.ts:103`*

```typescript
Conduit
```

The Conduit Protocol interface — high-level agent messaging.  Conduit wraps a Transport adapter, adding messaging semantics.  Implementations: - `ConduitClient` (`@cleocode/core` — wraps any Transport) - `HttpTransport` (HTTP polling to cloud SignalDock API) - `LocalTransport` (napi-rs in-process — embedded SignalDock, future) - `SseTransport` (Server-Sent Events — real-time cloud, future)  Consumers: - `@cleocode/cleo` CLI (`cleo agent watch/poll/send`) - `@cleocode/runtime` (background polling, SSE connections) - CleoOS Electron (embedded SignalDock via LocalTransport) - Agent spawners (deliver task assignments, collect results)

#### `send()`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/conduit.ts:107`*

```typescript
(to: string, content: string, options?: ConduitSendOptions) => Promise<ConduitSendResult>
```

Send a message to an agent or group.

#### `onMessage()`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/conduit.ts:110`*

```typescript
(handler: (message: ConduitMessage) => void) => ConduitUnsubscribe
```

Subscribe to incoming messages. Returns unsubscribe function.

#### `poll()`

### `Task`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:132`*

```typescript
Task
```

A single CLEO task as stored in the database.  Fields marked as required are enforced by CLEO's anti-hallucination validation at runtime. Making them required here ensures the type system catches violations at compile time rather than deferring to runtime checks.

#### `id`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:134`*

```typescript
string
```

Unique task identifier. Must match pattern `T\d{3,}` (e.g., T001, T5800).

#### `title`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:137`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable task title. Required, max 120 characters.

#### `description`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:143`*

```typescript
string
```

Task description. **Required** — CLEO's anti-hallucination rules reject tasks without a description, and require it to differ from the title.

#### `status`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:146`*

```typescript
"active" | "pending" | "cancelled" | "blocked" | "done" | "archived"
```

Current task status. Must be a valid `TaskStatus` enum value.

#### `priority`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:149`*

```typescript
TaskPriority
```

Task priority level. Defaults to `'medium'` on creation.

#### `type`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:152`*

```typescript
TaskType | undefined
```

Task type in hierarchy. Inferred from parent context if not specified.

#### `parentId`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:155`*

```typescript
string | null | undefined
```

ID of the parent task. `null` for root-level tasks.

#### `position`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:158`*

```typescript
number | null | undefined
```

Sort position within sibling scope.

#### `positionVersion`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:161`*

```typescript
number | undefined
```

Optimistic concurrency version for position changes.

#### `size`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:164`*

```typescript
TaskSize | null | undefined
```

Relative scope sizing (small/medium/large). NOT a time estimate.

#### `phase`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:167`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Phase slug this task belongs to.

#### `files`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:170`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

File paths associated with this task.

#### `acceptance`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:173`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Acceptance criteria for completion.

#### `depends`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:176`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

IDs of tasks this task depends on.

#### `relates`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:179`*

```typescript
TaskRelation[] | undefined
```

Related task entries (non-dependency relationships).

#### `epicLifecycle`

```typescript
EpicLifecycle | null | undefined
```

Epic lifecycle state. Only meaningful when `type = 'epic'`.

#### `noAutoComplete`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:185`*

```typescript
boolean | null | undefined
```

When true, epic will not auto-complete when all children are done.

#### `blockedBy`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:188`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Reason the task is blocked (free-form text).

#### `notes`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:191`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Timestamped notes appended during task lifecycle.

#### `labels`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:194`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Classification labels for filtering and grouping.

#### `origin`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:197`*

```typescript
TaskOrigin | null | undefined
```

Task origin/provenance category.

#### `createdAt`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:200`*

```typescript
string
```

ISO 8601 timestamp of task creation. Must not be in the future.

#### `updatedAt`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:203`*

```typescript
string | null | undefined
```

ISO 8601 timestamp of last update. Set automatically on mutation.

#### `completedAt`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:211`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

ISO 8601 timestamp of task completion. Set when `status` transitions to `'done'`. See `CompletedTask` for the status-narrowed type where this is required.

#### `cancelledAt`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:219`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

ISO 8601 timestamp of task cancellation. Set when `status` transitions to `'cancelled'`. See `CancelledTask` for the status-narrowed type where this is required.

#### `cancellationReason`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:227`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Reason for cancellation. Required when `status = 'cancelled'`. See `CancelledTask` for the status-narrowed type where this is required.

#### `verification`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:230`*

```typescript
TaskVerification | null | undefined
```

Verification pipeline state.

#### `provenance`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:233`*

```typescript
TaskProvenance | null | undefined
```

Provenance tracking (who created/modified, which session).

#### `pipelineStage`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:245`*

```typescript
string | null | undefined
```

RCASD-IVTR+C pipeline stage this task is associated with.  Valid values: research, consensus, architecture\_decision, specification, decomposition, implementation, validation, testing, release, contribution.  Auto-assigned on creation; only moves forward through stages.  T060

```typescript
string | null | undefined
```

Agent ID that has claimed/is assigned to this task. Null when unclaimed.

### `TaskCreate`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:265`*

```typescript
TaskCreate
```

Input type for creating a new task via `addTask()`.  Only the fields the caller MUST provide are required. All other fields have sensible defaults applied by the creation logic: - `status` defaults to `'pending'` - `priority` defaults to `'medium'` - `type` is inferred from parent context - `size` defaults to `'medium'`

#### `title`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:267`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable task title. Required, max 120 characters.

#### `description`

```typescript
string
```

Task description. **Required** — CLEO's anti-hallucination rules reject tasks without a description, and require it to differ from the title.

#### `status`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:276`*

```typescript
"active" | "pending" | "cancelled" | "blocked" | "done" | "archived" | undefined
```

Initial status. Defaults to `'pending'`.

#### `priority`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:279`*

```typescript
TaskPriority | undefined
```

Priority level. Defaults to `'medium'`.

#### `type`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:282`*

```typescript
TaskType | undefined
```

Task type. Inferred from parent context if not specified.

#### `parentId`

```typescript
string | null | undefined
```

Parent task ID for hierarchy placement.

#### `size`

```typescript
TaskSize | undefined
```

Relative scope sizing. Defaults to `'medium'`.

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Phase slug to assign. Inherited from project.currentPhase if not specified.

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Classification labels.

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

File paths associated with this task.

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Acceptance criteria.

IDs of tasks this task depends on.

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts:142`*

```typescript
SharingMode
```

Sharing mode (default: 'none').

#### `commitAllowlist`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts:144`*

```typescript
string[]
```

#### `denylist`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts:146`*

```typescript
string[]
```

### `BrainMemoryBridgeConfig`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts:156`*

```typescript
BrainMemoryBridgeConfig
```

Brain memory bridge refresh configuration. Controls when `.cleo/memory-bridge.md` is automatically regenerated.   T134  T135

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts:158`*

```typescript
boolean
```

Whether to automatically regenerate memory-bridge.md on lifecycle events (default: true).

#### `contextAware`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts:160`*

```typescript
boolean
```

Whether to include scope-aware memory context in generated bridge (default: false).

#### `maxTokens`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/config.ts:162`*

```typescript
number
```

Maximum token budget for memory bridge content (default: 2000).


#### `claimTask()`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/data-accessor.ts:275`*

```typescript
(taskId: string, agentId: string) => Promise<void>
```

Atomically claim a task for an agent.  Uses `UPDATE ... WHERE assignee IS NULL OR assignee = agentId` to prevent race conditions. Throws if the task is already claimed by a different agent.

**Parameters**

- `taskId` — ID of the task to claim.
- `agentId` — Agent identifier claiming the task.

**Throws**

- Error When the task is not found or is already claimed by another agent.

#### `unclaimTask()`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/data-accessor.ts:285`*

```typescript
(taskId: string) => Promise<void>
```

Release a claimed task, clearing its assignee.  No-op if the task is not currently claimed.

**Parameters**

- `taskId` — ID of the task to unclaim.

**Throws**

- Error When the task is not found.

### `RiskFactor`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:25`*

```typescript
RiskFactor
```

A single factor contributing to a task's overall risk score.  Each factor has a weight (how much it matters) and a value (current level, 0-1). The weighted sum of all factors produces the aggregate risk score.

#### `name`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:27`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable factor name (e.g., "complexity", "blocking\_risk").

#### `weight`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:29`*

```typescript
number
```

How much this factor contributes to the total score (0-1).

#### `value`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:31`*

```typescript
number
```

Current measured value for this factor (0-1, where 1 = highest risk).

#### `description`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:33`*

```typescript
string
```

Explanation of why this factor has its current value.

### `RiskAssessment`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:42`*

```typescript
RiskAssessment
```

Complete risk assessment for a single task.  Returned by `calculateTaskRisk`. The `riskScore` is the weighted aggregate of all `RiskFactor` entries in `factors`.

#### `taskId`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:44`*

```typescript
string
```

The task ID this assessment applies to.

#### `riskScore`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:46`*

```typescript
number
```

Aggregate risk score (0-1, where 1 = highest risk).

#### `confidence`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:48`*

```typescript
number
```

Confidence in the assessment (0-1). Higher when more data is available.

#### `factors`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:50`*

```typescript
RiskFactor[]
```

Individual risk factors that contributed to the score.

#### `recommendation`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:52`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable recommendation based on the risk level.

### `ValidationPrediction`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:61`*

```typescript
ValidationPrediction
```

Predicted outcome for a lifecycle validation gate.  Returned by `predictValidationOutcome`. Combines historical pattern data with the task's current state to estimate pass likelihood.

#### `taskId`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:63`*

```typescript
string
```

The task ID being evaluated.

#### `stage`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:65`*

```typescript
string
```

The lifecycle stage being predicted (e.g., "specification", "implementation").

#### `passLikelihood`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:67`*

```typescript
number
```

Probability of passing the gate (0-1).

#### `blockers`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:69`*

```typescript
string[]
```

Known blockers that may prevent passing.

#### `suggestions`

```typescript
string[]
```

### `DetectedPattern`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:84`*

```typescript
DetectedPattern
```

A pattern automatically detected from historical brain/task data.  Detected patterns may be stored in the existing brain\_patterns table if they meet frequency and confidence thresholds.

#### `type`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:86`*

```typescript
"workflow" | "success" | "failure" | "blocker" | "optimization"
```

```typescript
string
```

```typescript
number
```

```typescript
number | null
```

```typescript
"high" | "medium" | "low"
```

If this is an anti-pattern, describe the negative behavior.

```typescript
string | null
```

Recommended mitigation for anti-patterns.

```typescript
string[]
```

Confidence in this pattern's validity (0-1).

```typescript
PatternMatch

```

```typescript
string
```

The type of change being analyzed.

```typescript
AffectedTask[]
```

Tasks affected by this change, with predicted new status.

#### `cascadeDepth`

```typescript
number
```

Maximum depth of cascading effects in the dependency graph.

#### `recommendation`

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable recommendation based on the analysis.

### `ImpactedTask`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:258`*

```typescript
ImpactedTask
```

A single task predicted to be affected by a free-text change description.  Produced by `predictImpact` after matching candidate tasks against the change description and running downstream dependency analysis.

#### `id`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:260`*

```typescript
string
```

Task ID.

#### `title`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:263`*

```typescript
string
```

Task title.

#### `status`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:266`*

```typescript
string
```

Current task status.

#### `priority`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:269`*

```typescript
string
```

Current task priority.

#### `exposure`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:278`*

```typescript
"direct" | "dependent" | "transitive"
```

Severity estimate for this task's exposure to the change.  - `direct` — task title/description matched the change description - `dependent` — task depends on a matched task - `transitive` — downstream of a dependent via the dependency graph

#### `downstreamCount`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:284`*

```typescript
number
```

Number of downstream tasks that depend on this task. Higher values indicate higher cascading risk.

#### `reason`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:287`*

```typescript
string
```

Why this task is predicted to be affected.

### `ImpactReport`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:296`*

```typescript
ImpactReport
```

Full impact prediction report for a free-text change description.  Returned by `predictImpact`. Combines fuzzy task search with reverse dependency analysis to enumerate which tasks are at risk.

#### `change`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:298`*

```typescript
string
```

The original free-text change description.

#### `matchedTasks`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:304`*

```typescript
ImpactedTask[]
```

Tasks directly matched by the change description (fuzzy search). These are the "seed" tasks from which downstream impact is traced.

#### `affectedTasks`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:311`*

```typescript
ImpactedTask[]
```

All tasks predicted to be affected, ordered by exposure severity (direct first, then dependents, then transitive) and then by descending downstream count.

#### `totalAffected`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:316`*

```typescript
number
```

Total count of distinct affected tasks (including direct matches).

#### `summary`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:321`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable summary of predicted impact scope.

### `BlastRadius`

```typescript
BlastRadius
```

Quantified scope of a task's impact across the project.

#### `directCount`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:336`*

```typescript
number
```

Number of direct dependents.

#### `transitiveCount`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:339`*

```typescript
number
```

Number of transitive dependents (full downstream tree).

#### `epicCount`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/types.ts:342`*

```typescript
number
```

Number of distinct epics affected.

#### `projectPercentage`

```typescript
number
```

Percentage of the total project impacted (0-100).

#### `severity`


Classification of impact severity.

```typescript
VerificationConfidenceScore
```

```typescript
string
```

Task ID.

#### `confidenceScore`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/intelligence/adaptive-validation.ts:101`*

```typescript
number
```

Computed confidence score (0-1).  Score derivation:   - Gates passed vs required gates: up to 0.6   - Failure log length (fewer failures = higher confidence): up to 0.2   - Round number (fewer rounds = higher confidence): up to 0.2

```typescript
boolean
```

```typescript
string | undefined
```

*Defined in `packages/core/src/nexus/sharing/index.ts:36`*


**Examples**

```typescript
const status = await getSharingStatus();
if (status.hasGit && status.pendingChanges) {
  console.log('Uncommitted changes in .cleo/ — run: cleo checkpoint');
}
```

### `RetryOptions`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lib/retry.ts:33`*

```typescript
RetryOptions
```

Options that control retry behavior for `withRetry`.

#### `maxAttempts`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lib/retry.ts:39`*

```typescript
number | undefined
```

Maximum total number of attempts (initial + retries).   3

#### `baseDelayMs`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lib/retry.ts:47`*

```typescript
number | undefined
```

Delay before the second attempt in milliseconds. Each subsequent delay is `baseDelayMs * 2^(attempt - 1)`.   2000

#### `maxDelayMs`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lib/retry.ts:55`*

```typescript
number | undefined
```

Upper bound on computed delay in milliseconds. Prevents unbounded growth with many retries.   30000

#### `retryableErrors`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lib/retry.ts:65`*

```typescript
readonly RetryablePredicate[] | undefined
```

Explicit list of patterns or predicates that identify retryable errors.  When provided, ONLY errors matching at least one entry are retried. Errors that match none of the entries cause immediate failure.  When omitted, all errors are treated as retryable (up to `maxAttempts`).

### `RetryContext`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lib/retry.ts:74`*

```typescript
RetryContext
```

Metadata attached to errors thrown after all retry attempts are exhausted.  The last error from the final attempt is augmented with these fields so callers can distinguish a retry-exhausted failure from a first-attempt one.

#### `attempts`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lib/retry.ts:76`*

```typescript
number
```

Total number of attempts made (always equal to `maxAttempts`).

#### `totalDelayMs`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lib/retry.ts:78`*

```typescript
number
```

Cumulative delay applied across all retry waits in milliseconds.

### `SkillFrontmatter`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:73`*

```typescript
SkillFrontmatter
```

Skill frontmatter parsed from SKILL.md YAML header.  This is CLEO's extended skill metadata -- a functional superset of CAAMP's `SkillMetadata`. All fields from `CaampSkillMetadata` (name, description, version, allowedTools) are present here. CLEO adds: tags, triggers, dispatchPriority, model, invocable, command, protocol.  Use this type when working with CLEO's skill loading, dispatch, and orchestration systems. Use `CaampSkillMetadata` when interfacing directly with CAAMP's discovery/install APIs.

#### `name`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:75`*

```typescript
string
```

Skill name (lowercase, hyphens). Maps to CaampSkillMetadata.name.

#### `description`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:77`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable description. Maps to CaampSkillMetadata.description.

#### `version`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:79`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Semantic version string. Maps to CaampSkillMetadata.version.

#### `author`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:81`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Skill author (CLEO extension).

#### `tags`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:83`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Classification tags for search/filter (CLEO extension).

#### `triggers`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:85`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Trigger patterns for auto-dispatch (CLEO extension).

#### `dispatchPriority`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:87`*

```typescript
number | undefined
```

Priority for dispatch selection (CLEO extension).

#### `model`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:89`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Preferred LLM model (CLEO extension).

#### `allowedTools`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:91`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Allowed tool names. Maps to CaampSkillMetadata.allowedTools.

#### `invocable`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:93`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

Whether the skill can be invoked directly (CLEO extension).

#### `command`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:95`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

CLI command for direct invocation (CLEO extension).

#### `protocol`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/skills/types.ts:97`*

```typescript
SkillProtocolType | undefined
```

RCASD-IVTR+C protocol type (CLEO extension).

```typescript
Skill
```

*Defined in `packages/core/src/backfill/index.ts:25`*

```typescript
BackfillOptions
```

Options for backfillTasks().

#### `dryRun`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/backfill/index.ts:27`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

Preview changes only — do not modify the database. Default: false.

#### `rollback`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/backfill/index.ts:29`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

Revert a previous backfill (remove auto-generated AC + verification). Default: false.

*Defined in `packages/core/src/backfill/index.ts:31`*

```typescript
string[] | undefined
```

Restrict backfill to specific task IDs. Default: all tasks.


Results from the temporal decay step.

```typescript
BrainMaintenanceConsolidationResult
```

Results from the memory consolidation step.

#### `reconciliation`

```typescript
BrainMaintenanceReconciliationResult
```

Results from the cross-DB orphaned reference reconciliation step.

#### `embeddings`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/memory/brain-maintenance.ts:78`*

```typescript
BrainMaintenanceEmbeddingsResult
```

Results from the embedding backfill step.

#### `duration`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/memory/brain-maintenance.ts:80`*

```typescript
number
```

Total wall-clock duration of the maintenance run in milliseconds.

### `BrainMaintenanceOptions`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/memory/brain-maintenance.ts:89`*

```typescript
BrainMaintenanceOptions
```

Options for `runBrainMaintenance`.  All `skip*` flags default to `false` — the full maintenance pass runs unless specific steps are disabled.

#### `skipDecay`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/memory/brain-maintenance.ts:91`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

Skip the temporal decay step. Default: false.

#### `skipConsolidation`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/memory/brain-maintenance.ts:93`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

Skip the memory consolidation step. Default: false.

#### `skipReconciliation`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/memory/brain-maintenance.ts:95`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

Skip the cross-DB orphaned reference reconciliation step. Default: false.

#### `skipEmbeddings`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/memory/brain-maintenance.ts:97`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

Skip the embedding backfill step. Default: false.

#### `onProgress`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/memory/brain-maintenance.ts:106`*

```typescript
((step: string, current: number, total: number) => void) | undefined
```

Progress callback invoked before each step starts and after completion of each sub-item.

**Parameters**

- `step` — Human-readable step name (e.g. "decay", "consolidation", "embeddings")
- `current` — Items processed so far within the current step (0 before step starts)
- `total` — Total items expected for the current step (0 if unknown before start)

### `ApprovalToken`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:91`*

```typescript
ApprovalToken
```

An approval token for human-in-the-loop workflow gates.  Tokens are bound to a specific session and workflow. The `workflowHash` provides TOCTOU protection: if the workflow is modified between token creation and approval, the token is invalidated.  Security invariants: - Token values MUST NOT appear in audit logs, summaries, or error messages. - `approvalTokensJson` is NEVER included in handoff/debrief serialization. - Status transitions use atomic CAS (pending - approved|rejected|expired only).

#### `token`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:93`*

```typescript
string
```

UUID v4 generated via CSPRNG (crypto.randomUUID).

#### `sessionId`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:95`*

```typescript
string
```

The session that generated this token. Token is bound to this session.

#### `workflowName`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:97`*

```typescript
string
```

The workflow that contains the approval gate.

#### `gateName`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:99`*

```typescript
string
```

The name label of the specific approval gate.

#### `workflowHash`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:104`*

```typescript
string
```

SHA-256 hash of the workflow definition text at the time the token was created. Used for TOCTOU protection.

#### `message`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:106`*

```typescript
string
```

The message displayed to the approver.

#### `createdAt`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:108`*

```typescript
string
```

ISO 8601 timestamp of token creation.

#### `expiresAt`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:110`*

```typescript
string
```

ISO 8601 timestamp of token expiration. REQUIRED. Default 24h.

#### `status`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:112`*

```typescript
ApprovalTokenStatus
```

Current token state.

#### `approvedBy`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:114`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Identifier of the actor who approved/rejected (informational).

#### `approvedAt`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:116`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

ISO 8601 timestamp of the approval/rejection action.

#### `usedAt`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:118`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

ISO 8601 timestamp of when the token was consumed by the runtime.

#### `requestedBy`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/types.ts:120`*

```typescript
string
```

Identifier of the agent/workflow that requested approval.

#### `evaluate()`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/cant/discretion.ts:28`*

```typescript
(condition: string, context: DiscretionContext) => Promise<boolean>
```

Evaluate whether a discretion condition is met.

**Parameters**

- `condition` — The prose text between `**` delimiters.
- `context` — Execution context including session, variables, and prior results.

**Returns**: `true` if the condition is judged to be met.

```typescript
AgentCard
```

```typescript
  version: "1.0.0",
  url: "https://api.example.com",
  capabilities: { streaming: false },
  defaultInputModes: ["application/json"],
  defaultOutputModes: ["application/json"],
  skills: [],
};
```

### `DiscoveryConfig`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/discovery.ts:397`*

```typescript
DiscoveryConfig
```

Configuration for the discovery middleware (A2A v1.0 format).

**Examples**

```typescript
const config: DiscoveryConfig = {
  agent: {
    name: "my-agent",
    description: "Example",
    version: "1.0.0",
    url: "https://api.example.com",
    capabilities: { streaming: false },
    defaultInputModes: ["application/json"],
    defaultOutputModes: ["application/json"],
    skills: [],
  },
  cacheMaxAge: 3600,
};
```

#### `tty`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/types.ts:491`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

When true, indicates the output is connected to an interactive terminal. If no explicit format flag or project/user default is set, TTY terminals default to `"human"` format while non-TTY (piped, CI, agents) defaults to `"json"` per the LAFS protocol.  CLI tools should pass `process.stdout.isTTY ?? false` here.

#### `quiet`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/types.ts:497`*

```typescript
boolean | undefined
```

Suppress non-essential output for scripting. When true, only essential data is returned.

### `DeprecationEntry`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/deprecationRegistry.ts:23`*

```typescript
DeprecationEntry
```

A single deprecation rule in the registry.

**Examples**

```typescript
const entry: DeprecationEntry = {
  id: "meta-mvi-boolean",
  code: "W_DEPRECATED_META_MVI_BOOLEAN",
  message: "_meta.mvi boolean values are deprecated",
  deprecated: "1.0.0",
  replacement: "Use _meta.mvi as one of: minimal|standard|full|custom",
  removeBy: "2.0.0",
  detector: (env) => typeof env._meta.mvi === "boolean",
};
```

#### `id`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/deprecationRegistry.ts:25`*

```typescript
string
```

Unique identifier for this deprecation rule

#### `code`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/deprecationRegistry.ts:27`*

```typescript
string
```

Warning code emitted when detected

#### `message`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/deprecationRegistry.ts:29`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable deprecation message

#### `deprecated`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/deprecationRegistry.ts:31`*

```typescript
string
```

Version where the feature was deprecated

#### `replacement`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/deprecationRegistry.ts:36`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

Suggested replacement or migration path.

#### `removeBy`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/deprecationRegistry.ts:38`*

```typescript
string
```

Version where the deprecated feature will be removed

#### `detector`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/deprecationRegistry.ts:40`*

```typescript
(envelope: LAFSEnvelope) => boolean
```

Predicate that returns `true` when the envelope uses the deprecated feature


Input for creating a failing LAFS envelope.

```typescript
false
```

Discriminant marking this as an error input.

#### `error`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/envelope.ts:148`*

```typescript
Partial<LAFSError> & Pick<LAFSError, "message" | "code">
```

Partial error object; at minimum `code` and `message` are required.

#### `result`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/envelope.ts:160`*

```typescript
Record<string, unknown> | Record<string, unknown>[] | null | undefined
```

Optional result payload to include alongside the error. For validation tools (linters, type checkers), the actionable data (what to fix, suggested fixes) IS the result even when the operation "fails". Setting this allows agents to access both the error metadata and the detailed result in a single response.  When omitted or null, the envelope emits `result: null` (default behavior).

#### `page`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/envelope.ts:166`*

```typescript
import("/mnt/projects/cleocode/packages/lafs/src/types").LAFSPage | null | undefined
```

Pagination metadata, if applicable even in error scenarios.

```typescript
Record<string, unknown> | undefined
```

**Examples**

```typescript
const pd: LafsProblemDetails = {
  type: "https://lafs.dev/errors/v1/E_VALIDATION",
  title: "E_VALIDATION",
  status: 400,
  detail: "Invalid input",
  retryable: false,
};
```

```typescript
string
```

URI reference identifying the problem type

```typescript
string
```

Short human-readable summary (typically the error code)

```typescript
number
```

HTTP status code for this error

#### `detail`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/problemDetails.ts:37`*

```typescript
string
```

Human-readable explanation of the specific occurrence

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/problemDetails.ts:42`*

```typescript
string | undefined
```

URI reference identifying the specific occurrence (typically the request ID).

#### `retryable`

*Defined in `packages/lafs/src/problemDetails.ts:44`*

```typescript
boolean
```

Whether the operation that caused this error can be retried

```typescript
string | undefined
```

```typescript

```

#### `suggestedAction`

```typescript

```

#### `docUrl`

```typescript
string | undefined
```

### `ConfigFormat`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/types.ts:99`*

```typescript
ConfigFormat
```

Supported configuration file formats.  - `"json"` - Standard JSON - `"jsonc"` - JSON with comments (comment-preserving via jsonc-parser) - `"yaml"` - YAML (via js-yaml) - `"toml"` - TOML (via iarna/toml)

**Examples**

```typescript
const format: ConfigFormat = "jsonc";
```

### `TransportType`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/types.ts:117`*

```typescript
TransportType
```

MCP server transport protocol type.  - `"stdio"` - Standard input/output (local process) - `"sse"` - Server-Sent Events (remote) - `"http"` - HTTP/Streamable HTTP (remote)

**Examples**

```typescript
const transport: TransportType = "stdio";
```

### `DetectionMethod`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/types.ts:131`*

```typescript
DetectionMethod
```

Method used to detect whether an AI agent is installed on the system.  - `"binary"` - Check if a CLI binary exists on PATH - `"directory"` - Check if known config/data directories exist - `"appBundle"` - Check for macOS .app bundle in standard app directories - `"flatpak"` - Check for Flatpak installation on Linux

### `ProviderPriority`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/types.ts:274`*

```typescript
ProviderPriority
```

Priority tier for a provider, used for sorting and default selection.  - `"high"` - Major, widely-used agents - `"medium"` - Established but less common agents - `"low"` - Niche or experimental agents

### `ProviderStatus`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/types.ts:286`*

```typescript
ProviderStatus
```

Lifecycle status of a provider in the registry.  - `"active"` - Fully supported - `"beta"` - Supported but may have rough edges - `"deprecated"` - Still present but no longer recommended - `"planned"` - Not yet implemented

### `SourceType`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/types.ts:437`*

```typescript
SourceType
```

Classified type of an MCP server or skill source.  - `"remote"` - HTTP/HTTPS URL to a remote MCP server - `"package"` - npm package name - `"command"` - Shell command string - `"github"` - GitHub repository (URL or shorthand) - `"gitlab"` - GitLab repository URL - `"local"` - Local filesystem path - `"library"` - Built-in skill library reference

### `AuditSeverity`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/types.ts:788`*

```typescript
AuditSeverity
```

Severity level for a security audit finding.  Ordered from most to least severe: `"critical"`  `"high"`  `"medium"`  `"low"`  `"info"`.

### `InjectionStatus`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/types.ts:905`*

```typescript
InjectionStatus
```

Status of a CAAMP injection block in an instruction file.  - `"current"` - Injection block exists and matches expected content - `"outdated"` - Injection block exists but content differs - `"missing"` - Instruction file does not exist - `"none"` - File exists but has no CAAMP injection block

### `PathScope`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/core/paths/standard.ts:16`*

```typescript
PathScope
```

Scope for path resolution, either global (user home) or project-local.

### `MVILevel`

*Defined in `packages/caamp/src/core/lafs.ts:22`*

```typescript
MVILevel
```

LAFS MVI disclosure level - defined locally to avoid CI module resolution issues with re-exported types.

```typescript
LAFSError
```

### `ConflictPolicy`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task-sync.ts:95`*

```typescript
ConflictPolicy
```

Policy for resolving conflicts between CLEO and provider state.  - `cleo-wins`: CLEO state takes precedence (default). - `provider-wins`: Provider state takes precedence. - `latest-wins`: Most recently modified value wins. - `report-only`: Report conflicts without applying changes.

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:322`*

```typescript
CompletedTask
```

A task with `status = 'done'`. Narrows `Task` to require `completedAt`.  Use this type when you need to guarantee a completed task has its completion timestamp — for example, in cycle-time calculations or archive operations.

### `CancelledTask`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/task.ts:336`*

```typescript
CancelledTask
```

A task with `status = 'cancelled'`. Narrows `Task` to require `cancelledAt` and `cancellationReason`.  Use this type when processing cancelled tasks where the cancellation metadata is guaranteed to be present.

## Enums

### `ExitCode`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/exit-codes.ts:11`*

```typescript
ExitCode
```

CLEO exit codes — canonical definitions shared across all layers.  Ranges: 0 = success, 1-99 = errors, 100+ = special (non-error) states.   T4454  T4456  T5710

### `OrchestrationLevel`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/orchestration-hierarchy.ts:20`*

```typescript
OrchestrationLevel
```

The 5 orchestration levels in order of authority.

#### `HITL`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/orchestration-hierarchy.ts:22`*

```typescript
OrchestrationLevel.HITL
```

Level 0: Human owner. Final authority. Never contacted by agents directly.

#### `Prime`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/orchestration-hierarchy.ts:24`*

```typescript
OrchestrationLevel.Prime
```

Level 1: Prime Orchestrator. Cross-project coordination. Breaks ties.

#### `ProjectLead`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/orchestration-hierarchy.ts:26`*

```typescript
OrchestrationLevel.ProjectLead
```

Level 2: Project Lead. Architecture decisions within a project.

#### `TeamLead`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/orchestration-hierarchy.ts:28`*

```typescript
OrchestrationLevel.TeamLead
```

Level 3: Team Lead. Task management. Can spawn ephemeral agents.

#### `Ephemeral`

*Defined in `packages/contracts/src/orchestration-hierarchy.ts:30`*

```typescript
OrchestrationLevel.Ephemeral
```

Level 4: Ephemeral agent. Task-scoped, short-lived.

### `ProtocolExitCode`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/compliance/protocol-types.ts:49`*

```typescript
ProtocolExitCode
```

Protocol-specific exit codes used by the RCASD-IVTR+C enforcement system.  These map to the protocol violation range (60-70) and lifecycle enforcement range (80-84) defined in the protocol specification. The values here align with the protocol enforcement layer's own exit code semantics, which differ from the canonical CLI exit codes in src/types/exit-codes.ts.  The canonical CLI ExitCode enum at src/types/exit-codes.ts maps range 60-67 to orchestrator errors, while this enum maps them to protocol violations. Both are valid in their respective contexts — CLI vs protocol enforcement.

#### `SUCCESS`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/compliance/protocol-types.ts:51`*


Task change audit log — stores every add/update/complete/delete/archive operation. Migrated from legacy JSONL task logs to SQLite per ADR-006/ADR-012. No FK on taskId — log entries must survive task deletion.   T4837

Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) stored in the database. Corresponds to the physical ADR markdown files in .cleo/adrs/. Self-referential FKs (supersedes\_id, superseded\_by\_id, amends\_id) are enforced at the DB level by T033 migration.


Tracks links between CLEO tasks and external system tasks (Linear, Jira, GitHub, etc.). Used by the reconciliation engine to match external tasks to existing CLEO tasks, detect updates, and maintain bidirectional traceability.  Each row represents one link: one CLEO task ↔ one external task from one provider. A CLEO task MAY have links from multiple providers (e.g., both Linear and GitHub). An external task SHOULD have at most one link per provider.

### `CLEO_GITIGNORE_FALLBACK`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/scaffold.ts:71`*

```typescript
"# .cleo/.gitignore — Deny-by-default for CLEO project data\n# Ignore everything, then explicitly allow only tracked files.\n\n# Step 1: Ignore everything\n*\n\n# Allow list\n!.gitignore\n!config.json\n!project-context.json\n!project-info.json\n!setup-otel.sh\n!DATA-SAFETY-IMPLEMENTATION-SUMMARY.md\n!adrs/\n!adrs/**\n!rcasd/\n!rcasd/**\n!agent-outputs/\n!agent-outputs/**\n\n# Explicit deny safety net\n*.db\n*.db-shm\n*.db-wal\n*.db-journal\nlog.json\nbypass-log.json\nqa-log.json\n.deps-cache/\n.context-alert-state.json\n.context-state*.json\ncontext-states/\n.git-checkpoint-state\n.migration-state.json\nmigrations.json\nsync/\nmetrics/\n.backups/\nbackups/\n"
```

Embedded fallback for .cleo/.gitignore content (deny-by-default).

### `REQUIRED_GLOBAL_SUBDIRS`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/scaffold.ts:1337`*

```typescript
readonly ["logs", "templates"]
```

Required subdirectories under the global \~/.cleo/ home. These are infrastructure directories managed by CLEO itself, not project-specific data.  Truly global:   - logs      — global log output   - templates — CLEO-INJECTION.md symlink target  Note: nexus.db lives directly in \~/.cleo/, not a subdir. Schemas are read at runtime from getPackageRoot()/schemas/ — no copy needed. Project-level dirs (adrs/, rcasd/, agent-outputs/, backups/) live in .cleo/ only.

### `STALE_GLOBAL_ENTRIES`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/scaffold.ts:1351`*

```typescript
readonly ["adrs", "rcasd", "agent-outputs", "backups", "sandbox", "tasks.db", "tasks.db-shm", "tasks.db-wal", "brain-worker.pid", "VERSION", "schemas", "bin", ".install-state", "templates/templates"]
```

Stale entries that must NOT exist at the global \~/.cleo/ level. These were project-level artefacts accidentally placed in the global home by old versions of CLEO. They are safe to remove because:   - adrs/, rcasd/, agent-outputs/, backups/ — project-level, live in .cleo/   - sandbox/                                — unused   - tasks.db / tasks.db-shm / tasks.db-wal  — project-level database files   - brain-worker.pid                         — stale PID file   - VERSION                                  — redundant (read from package.json)   - schemas/                                 — now read from npm binary at runtime   - bin/                                     — stale dev symlink directory


### `CORE_PROTECTED_FILES`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/constants.ts:13`*

```typescript
readonly ["config.json", ".gitignore", "project-info.json", "project-context.json"]
```

Configuration files relative to .cleo/ that MUST remain tracked by project git. These are JSON/text config files only — NOT databases. SQLite databases (tasks.db) are excluded: they must be gitignored to prevent data-loss from merge conflicts (see ADR-013).  If any of these files are gitignored, cleo doctor reports a critical finding.

### `DEFAULT_PROTOCOL_STAGE_MAP`

*Defined in `packages/core/src/lifecycle/default-chain.ts:28`*

```typescript
Record<"research" | "consensus" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "release" | "contribution" | "provenance" | "artifact-publish", "research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release">
```

Stage mapping for protocol validation gates in the default chain.  `contribution` is cross-cutting and is validated at implementation. `artifact-publish` and `provenance` are validated at release.   T5419
